mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate decline instances.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion that drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns are negative in more than 50% of synchronized increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The mechanism produces negative forward returns in over 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3 percent annually, or the signal's predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real purchasing power and prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 60% of CPIAUCSL increase periods, or the rank correlation remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Gross domestic product growth dropping below 1.5 percent annually signals demand contraction that reduces earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in over 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below the threshold, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance at the 90% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regressions, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of divergence instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate cuts during periods of economic slowdown provide liquidity support that stabilizes equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in over 60% of rate cut instances during slowdown periods, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward tests.
